commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram reasoning draft dispatch so split reasoning clears the existing preview before forcing a new message, and adds a regression test for that ordering.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +33. Total +48 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main sets splitReasoningOnNextStream on reasoning end, then calls forceNewMessage before clearing the old Telegram preview id; I did not run a live Telegram DM reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual proof handling is needed because the remaining blocker is live Telegram evidence or maintainer acceptance, not a code defect ClawSweeper can repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): clear reasoning preview before forceNewMessage rotation (#80862)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #80862.

When `/reasoning stream` is enabled on Telegram, splitting reasoning across segments left stale reasoning preview messages in chat. The bug: `splitReasoningOnNextStream` gated a `forceNewMessage()` rotation that dropped the prior `streamMessageId` *before* `clear()` could delete the old preview, so the old message id was orphaned and never deleted.

## Why This Change Was Made

Root cause is the lifecycle ordering in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`. The previous code did:

```ts
if (splitReasoningOnNextStream) {
  reasoningLane.stream?.forceNewMessage();  // drops old streamMessageId
  resetDraftLaneState(reasoningLane);
  splitReasoningOnNextStream = false;
}
```

`forceNewMessage()` resets `streamMessageId` to `undefined` (per `draft-stream.ts:554`). After that, `clear()` cannot delete the old preview because it can no longer read the id.

The fix mirrors the existing `rotateAnswerLaneForNewMessage` shape but uses `clear()` (delete the old preview) instead of `stop()`:

```ts
const clearReasoningLaneForNewMessage = async () => {
  if (
    !reasoningLane.hasStreamedMessage &&
    typeof reasoningLane.stream?.messageId() !== "number"
  ) {
    resetDraftLaneState(reasoningLane);
    return;
  }
  await reasoningLane.stream?.clear();       // delete old preview using known id
  reasoningLane.stream?.forceNewMessage();   // then rotate to fresh stream
  resetDraftLaneState(reasoningLane);
};
```

This is the same fix shape ClawSweeper validated on the prior closed PR #94631 (closed by author, not by maintainer rejection). Issue labels `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:source-repro` confirm the implementation path.

## User Impact

Telegram users with `/reasoning stream` enabled will no longer see stale reasoning preview messages accumulate in chat when reasoning splits across segments. Only one reasoning preview message will exist at a time, matching the documented streaming behavior.

## Evidence

Local Vitest proof (deterministic, mock-based):

```
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
    Tests  137 passed (137)
```

New regression test `clears the prior reasoning preview before splitting into a new reasoning message` asserts the ordering invariant:
- `clear()` runs before `forceNewMessage()` (old id still known when delete fires)
- `clear()` runs before the next `update()` (new stream does not race ahead)
- `forceNewMessage()` runs exactly once per split

Typecheck and lint clean:
- `tsgo -p tsconfig.extensions.json`: clean
- `oxlint` on changed files: clean

Live Telegram DM proof is the merge gate per `.agents/maintainer-notes/telegram.md`. I do not have Telegram bot credentials in this environment; maintainer may request a live recording before merge.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
